description: Sample SSA event envelope for `garden.harvest.logged`. Emit this when a harvest is recorded from the garden module (manual entry, scan from garden task list, or auto-log after a scheduled task). This is the bridge from GARDEN → INVENTORY/STOREHOUSE → (optional) PRESERVATION and (optional) HUB export.

data.unit: string — lb | oz | bunch | head | basket | crate
data.inventoryItemId: string | null — where we deposited it (if already linked to inventory/storehouse)

samples.label: Simple greens harvest → goes straight to inventory
Herb harvest (rosemary) → can link to inventory OR to garden-only ledger
Bulk root crop harvest → likely needs storehouse + preservation
Leafy + medicinal garden (no inventory link yet) → just log it
Animal-feed harvest (for your agrarian + animal integration) → send to inventory as feed

notes: 1. This event is what the INVENTORY module should listen for to auto-create or auto-increment items. If an inventory item is created/updated, that module should emit `inventory.updated` right after.
2. If preservationSuggested=true, the preservation engine can emit `automation.schedule.request` to create a canning/dehydrating/fermenting task.

3. Because garden harvests produce household assets, this event is a good candidate to export if featureFlags.familyFundMode=true. A listener like src/events/eventMappers.js can map → hub:garden.harvest and send.
4. Keep this file JSON-only (no $schema) to avoid VS Code remote schema errors.
5. You can add more domains in linkHints-style later if you want harvests to directly inform meal planning (e.g. 'we harvested 18 lb tomatoes → suggest pasta sauce meal sessions').
